title: Cipher Mining Expands AI Data Centers, Secures New Financing
text: Cipher Mining (CIFR) announced first-quarter 2026 results featuring $35 million in revenue alongside an adjusted EBITDA loss of $48 million as the company continues aggressive expansion efforts. The company is advancing two major campus projects. Construction at Barber Lake reached the topping-out stage in April, while Black Pearl is progressing through Phase I retrofitting and Phase II site work activities. Management disclosed securing a third AI data center campus lease with an investment-grade hyperscale tenant and closing a $200 million revolving credit facility. These moves strengthen the company's liquidity position and support capital requirements for new campuses. The organization shifted its primary performance indicator from Adjusted Earnings to Adjusted EBITDA, reflecting growing interest expenses from recent convertible and senior secured note offerings. The most recent analyst rating maintains a Buy recommendation with a $23.00 price target, though TipRanks' AI analyst assigns a Neutral score, citing weak financial performance offset by credible movement toward long-duration contracted revenue with fully funded projects.
